value.claim: A pure Transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks without relying on convolutional neural networks.
Large-scale pre-training (14M-300M images) trumps the inductive biases inherent to CNNs, allowing Vision Transformers to approach or beat state-of-the-art performance.
The best Vision Transformer model (ViT-H/14) achieves 88.55% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet validation set.
Vision Transformers require substantially fewer computational resources to train compared to state-of-the-art convolutional networks while achieving comparable or better performance.
Vision Transformers dominate ResNets on the performance/compute trade-off, using approximately 2-4x less compute to attain the same performance.
